modules.name: dynamodb-table
modules.summary: This Terraform module provisions an AWS DynamoDB table with configurable attributes, billing mode, and key schema. The table's properties such as name, billing mode, hash key, range key, and attribute definitions are parameterized via input
modules.description: This Terraform module provisions an AWS DynamoDB table with configurable attributes, billing mode, and key schema. The table's properties such as name, billing mode, hash key, range key, and attribute definitions are parameterized via input

inputs.name: attributes
inputs.description: Information about attributes used in index (hash and range key) of the table in the format [{'name': 'field_name', 'type': 'field_type'}]. All indexes defined for the table (hash key and range key) must mandatorily be contained as elements of this attribute list.

validation.error_message: DynamoDB table attributes need to be defined with primitive types that refer to: 'S' for strings, 'N' for numeric and 'B' for binary.

inputs.name: billing_mode
inputs.description: Type of billing associated with consumption of items from the created table

validation.error_message: The variable value must be 'PROVISIONED' or 'PAY_PER_REQUEST'.

inputs.name: hash_key
inputs.description: Attribute name to be used as primary key (hash key) of the table

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the table to be created in DynamoDB

inputs.name: range_key
inputs.description: Attribute name to be used as second part of the secondary key (range key) of the table

inputs.name: stream_enabled
inputs.description: Indicates whether DynamoDB Streams is to be enabled (true) or disabled (false).

inputs.name: stream_view_type
inputs.description: When an item in the table is modified, StreamViewType determines what information is written to the stream for this table. Valid values are: 'NEW_IMAGE', 'OLD_IMAGE', 'NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES', 'KEYS_ONLY'.

validation.error_message: The variable value must be 'NEW_IMAGE', 'OLD_IMAGE', 'NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES', or 'KEYS_ONLY'.

inputs.name: tags
inputs.description: Tags to apply to the DynamoDB table.

outputs.name: stream_arn
outputs.description: ARN of the DynamoDB table stream.

examples.advanced:
module "dynamodb-table" {
  source = "github.com/ThiagoPanini/tfbox//aws/dynamodb-table"

  hash_key             = "hash_key-example"
  name                 = "name-example"
  billing_mode         = "PAY_PER_REQUEST"
  stream_enabled       = false
}

modules.name: iam-role
modules.summary: This Terraform file manages the declaration, rendering, and deployment of IAM policies to be associated with an IAM role. It automates the process of rendering user-defined policy templates, loading the rendered files, and creating AWS IAM
modules.description: This Terraform file manages the declaration, rendering, and deployment of IAM policies to be associated with an IAM role. It automates the process of rendering user-defined policy templates, loading the rendered files, and creating AWS IAM

inputs.name: policies_template_config
inputs.description: Configuration for IAM policy templates, including source and destination directories and template variables.

inputs.name: roles_config
inputs.description: List of configuration maps for each IAM role that should be created in a single module call, including role name, trust policy file path and associated policy ARNs.

inputs.name: tags
inputs.description: Tags to apply to the IAM role.

outputs.name: policies_arns
outputs.description: ARNs of the policies attached to the IAM roles.
outputs.expression: { for policy in aws_iam_policy.policies : policy.name => policy.arn }

outputs.name: roles_arns
outputs.description: ARNs of the created IAM roles.
outputs.expression: { for role in aws_iam_role.roles : role.name => role.arn }

resources.name: roles
resources.forEach: { for role in var.roles_config : role.role_name => role }

modules.name: lambda-function
modules.summary: This Terraform module provisions AWS Lambda functions with flexible configuration and best practices.
modules.description: This Terraform module provisions AWS Lambda functions with flexible configuration and best practices.

inputs.name: architectures
inputs.description: Lambda code architecture (e.g., x86_64, arm64)

validation.error_message: Lambda architectures must be either ['x86_64'] or ['arm64'].

inputs.name: create_eventbridge_trigger
inputs.description: Whether to create an EventBridge rule to trigger the Lambda function based on a cron expression.

inputs.name: cron_expression
inputs.description: Cron expression to schedule the Lambda function using EventBridge (e.g., cron(0 12 * * ? *)).

validation.error_message: If create_eventbridge_trigger is true, cron_expression must be a valid cron expression in the format cron(...).

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Description of the Lambda function

validation.error_message: Lambda function description must be 256 characters or fewer.

inputs.name: environment_variables
inputs.description: Map of environment variables to set for the Lambda function.

inputs.name: exclude_patterns
inputs.description: List of glob patterns for files/directories to exclude from source code hash calculation. These files won't trigger Lambda redeployment when changed.

inputs.name: function_name
inputs.description: Name of the Lambda function

validation.error_message: Lambda function name must be 1-64 characters long and consist only of alphanumeric characters, underscores, or hyphens.

inputs.name: lambda_handler
inputs.description: Lambda function handler (e.g., lambda_function.lambda_handler).

validation.error_message: Handler must be a valid Python module and function name (e.g., module.function).

inputs.name: layers_arns
inputs.description: List of ARNs for AWS managed Lambda Layers to attach to the function (e.g., AWS provided layers or layers from other accounts).

inputs.name: memory_size
inputs.description: Function memory size in MB from 128 MB to 10240 MB (10 GB)

validation.error_message: Memory size must be between 128 and 10240 MB.

inputs.name: role_arn
inputs.description: IAM role ARN for Lambda execution

validation.error_message: role_arn must be a valid IAM role ARN.

inputs.name: runtime
inputs.description: Lambda runtime (e.g., python3.13, nodejs22.x)

inputs.name: source_code_hash_enabled
inputs.description: Whether to enable source code hash calculation for change detection. If false, will use timestamp-based triggers (always redeploy).

inputs.name: source_code_path
inputs.description: The local path where the Lambda source code is located (e.g 'app', 'app/src' or 'app/src/my_app_slice').

validation.error_message: The source code path must not end with a slash ('/').

inputs.name: tags
inputs.description: Tags to apply to the Lambda function.

inputs.name: timeout
inputs.description: Function timeout in seconds

validation.error_message: Timeout must be between 1 and 900 seconds.

outputs.name: function_role_arn
outputs.description: IAM role ARN used by the Lambda function

examples.minimal:
module "lambda-function" {
  source = "github.com/ThiagoPanini/tfbox//aws/lambda-function"

  function_name        = "function_name-example"
  lambda_handler       = "lambda_handler-example"
  role_arn             = "role_arn-example"
  runtime              = "runtime-example"
  source_code_path     = "source_code_path-example"
}
examples.advanced:
module "lambda-function" {
  source = "github.com/ThiagoPanini/tfbox//aws/lambda-function"

  function_name        = "function_name-example"
  lambda_handler       = "lambda_handler-example"
  role_arn             = "role_arn-example"
  runtime              = "runtime-example"
  source_code_path     = "source_code_path-example"
  create_eventbridge_trigger = false
  cron_expression      = ""
  description          = ""
  memory_size          = 128
  source_code_hash_enabled = true
  timeout              = 60
}

modules.name: lambda-layer
modules.summary: This Terraform module creates AWS Lambda layers based on the provided configuration. It builds the layers from specified Python requirements, packages them into zip files, and deploys them as Lambda layer versions. The module supports clean
modules.description: This Terraform module creates AWS Lambda layers based on the provided configuration. It builds the layers from specified Python requirements, packages them into zip files, and deploys them as Lambda layer versions. The module supports clean

inputs.name: layers_config
inputs.description: A configuration object for the Lambda layers, including the mount point for building layers and whether to clean up temporary build directories after layer creation.

inputs.name: layers_programming_language
inputs.description: The programming language for the Lambda layers. Currently, only 'python' is supported.

validation.error_message: Currently, only 'python' is supported as the programming language for Lambda layers.

outputs.name: layers_arns
outputs.description: A map of Lambda layer ARNs created by this module, with layer names as keys.
outputs.expression: { for layer in aws_lambda_layer_version.this : layer.layer_name => layer.arn }

resources.name: this
resources.forEach: { for layer in var.layers_config : layer.name => layer }

resources.name: build_layer
resources.forEach: { for layer in var.layers_config : layer.name => layer }

resources.name: cleanup_layer_build
resources.forEach: { for layer in var.layers_config : layer.name => layer }

modules.name: sns-topic
modules.summary: This Terraform module provisions AWS SNS topics with flexible configuration options. It supports standard and FIFO topics, encryption, topic policies, subscriptions, tags, and data protection policies.
modules.description: This Terraform module provisions AWS SNS topics with flexible configuration options. It supports standard and FIFO topics, encryption, topic policies, subscriptions, tags, and data protection policies.

inputs.name: content_based_deduplication
inputs.description: Enable content-based deduplication for FIFO topics.

inputs.name: create_data_protection_policy
inputs.description: Whether to create a data protection policy.

inputs.name: create_topic_policy
inputs.description: Whether to create a topic policy.

inputs.name: data_protection_policy
inputs.description: Inline data protection policy JSON.

validation.error_message: When var.create_data_protection_policy is true and var.data_protection_policy_file_path is null, then you're setting a data protection policy using an inline policy and so var.data_protection_policy must be a valid JSON string.

inputs.name: data_protection_policy_file_path
inputs.description: Path to data protection policy JSON file.

validation.error_message: When var.create_data_protection_policy is true and var.data_protection_policy is null, then you're setting a data protection policy using a file and so var.data_protection_policy_file_path must be a valid file path.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.description: Display name for the SNS topic.

validation.error_message: Display name must have a maximum length of 100 characters.

inputs.name: fifo_topic
inputs.description: Whether this is a FIFO topic.

inputs.name: kms_master_key_id
inputs.description: KMS key ID for encryption.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the SNS topic.

validation.error_message: SNS topic name must consist of alphanumeric characters, underscores, or hyphens.

inputs.name: subscriptions
inputs.description: List of subscription objects.

validation.error_message: Each subscription protocol must be one of: http, https, sqs, email, lambda.

inputs.name: tags
inputs.description: Tags to apply to the SNS topic.

inputs.name: topic_policy
inputs.description: Inline topic policy JSON.

validation.error_message: If create_topic_policy is true and topic_policy is set, it must be a valid JSON string.

inputs.name: topic_policy_file_path
inputs.description: Path to topic policy JSON file.

validation.error_message: If create_topic_policy is true and topic_policy_file_path is set, it must be a valid file path.

examples.advanced:
module "sns-topic" {
  source = "github.com/ThiagoPanini/tfbox//aws/sns-topic"

  name                 = "name-example"
  content_based_deduplication = false
  create_data_protection_policy = false
  create_topic_policy  = false
  fifo_topic           = false
}

modules.name: sqs-queue
modules.summary: This Terraform module provisions AWS SQS queues with flexible configuration options. It supports both standard and FIFO queues, optional dead-letter queues (DLQ), encryption (SSE-SQS or KMS), configurable access policies (inline or file-bas
modules.description: This Terraform module provisions AWS SQS queues with flexible configuration options. It supports both standard and FIFO queues, optional dead-letter queues (DLQ), encryption (SSE-SQS or KMS), configurable access policies (inline or file-bas

inputs.name: access_policy
inputs.description: The access policy for the SQS queue. This is required if access_policy_configuration_type is 'inline'.

validation.error_message: Access policy must be provided if create_access_policy is true and access_policy_configuration_type is 'inline'.

inputs.name: access_policy_configuration_type
inputs.description: Type of access policy configuration. Can be 'inline' or 'file'.

validation.error_message: Access policy configuration type must be either 'inline' or 'file'.

inputs.name: access_policy_file_path
inputs.description: The file path for the access policy if access_policy_configuration_type is 'file'.

validation.error_message: Access policy file path must be provided if create_access_policy is true and access_policy_configuration_type is 'file'.

inputs.name: content_based_deduplication
inputs.description: Enables content-based deduplication for FIFO queues.

inputs.name: copy_dlq_config_from_source_queue
inputs.description: Whether to copy the source queue's configuration to the dead-letter queue. If true, the DLQ will inherit the source queue's settings such as visibility timeout, message retention, etc. If false, users need to configure DLQ settings separately through variables provided.

inputs.name: create_access_policy
inputs.description: Boolean to enable creation of access policy for the SQS queue.

inputs.name: create_dead_letter_queue
inputs.description: Whether to create and attach a dead letter queue.

inputs.name: create_fifo_queue
inputs.description: Boolean to create FIFO queue.

inputs.name: deduplication_scope
inputs.description: The deduplication scope for FIFO queues. Can be 'messageGroup' or 'queue'.

validation.error_message: Deduplication scope must be either 'messageGroup' or 'queue'.

inputs.name: delay_seconds
inputs.description: The time, in seconds, that the delivery of all messages in the queue will be delayed. Any messages sent to the queue remain invisible to consumers for the duration of the delay period

validation.error_message: Delay seconds must be between 0 and 900 seconds (15 minutes).

inputs.name: dlq_content_based_deduplication
inputs.description: Enables content-based deduplication for DLQ FIFO queues.

inputs.name: dlq_deduplication_scope
inputs.description: The deduplication scope for DLQ FIFO queues. Can be 'messageGroup' or 'queue'.

validation.error_message: Deduplication scope must be either 'messageGroup' or 'queue'.

inputs.name: dlq_delay_seconds
inputs.description: The time, in seconds, that the delivery of all messages in the DLQ will be delayed. Any messages sent to the queue remain invisible to consumers for the duration of the delay period

validation.error_message: Delay seconds for DLQ must be between 0 and 900 seconds (15 minutes).

inputs.name: dlq_fifo_throughput_limit
inputs.description: The throughput limit for DLQ FIFO queues. Can be 'perMessageGroupId' or 'perQueue'.

validation.error_message: DLQ FIFO Throughput limit must be either 'perMessageGroupId' or 'perQueue'.

inputs.name: dlq_kms_data_key_reuse_period_seconds
inputs.description: The length of time, in seconds, for which the KMS data key is cached. This is only applicable if KMS master key is used.

validation.error_message: KMS data key reuse period for DLQ must be between 60 seconds (1 minute) and 86400 seconds (1 day).

inputs.name: dlq_kms_master_key_id
inputs.description: The ID of the KMS master key used for server-side encryption (SSE) if SQS managed SSE is not enabled for DLQ.

inputs.name: dlq_max_message_size
inputs.description: The limit of how many bytes a message can contain before Amazon SQS rejects it.

validation.error_message: Max message size must be between 1024 (1 KB) and 262144 bytes (256 KB).

inputs.name: dlq_message_retention_seconds
inputs.description: The length of time, in seconds, that Amazon SQS retains a message that does not get deleted.

validation.error_message: Message retention for DLQ must be between 60 seconds (1 minute) and 1209600 seconds (14 days).

inputs.name: dlq_receive_wait_time_seconds
inputs.description: The time for which a ReceiveMessage call will wait for a message to arrive. In other words, the maximum amount of time that polling will wait for messages to become available to receive.

validation.error_message: Receive wait time for DLQ must be between 0 and 20 seconds.

inputs.name: dlq_sqs_managed_sse_enabled
inputs.description: Boolean to enable SQS managed server-side encryption (SSE) for DLQ.

inputs.name: dlq_visibility_timeout_seconds
inputs.description: The visibility timeout for the queue (in seconds). Visibility timeout sets the length of time that a message received from a queue (by one consumer) will not be visible to the other message consumers.

validation.error_message: Visibility timeout for DLQ must be between 0 seconds and 43200 seconds (12 hours).

inputs.name: fifo_throughput_limit
inputs.description: The throughput limit for FIFO queues. Can be 'perMessageGroupId' or 'perQueue'.

validation.error_message: FIFO Throughput limit must be either 'perMessageGroupId' or 'perQueue'.

inputs.name: kms_data_key_reuse_period_seconds
inputs.description: The length of time, in seconds, for which the KMS data key is cached. This is only applicable if KMS master key is used.

validation.error_message: KMS data key reuse period must be between 60 seconds (1 minute) and 86400 seconds (1 day).

inputs.name: kms_master_key_id
inputs.description: The ID of the KMS master key used for server-side encryption (SSE) if SQS managed SSE is not enabled.

inputs.name: max_message_size
inputs.description: The limit of how many bytes a message can contain before Amazon SQS rejects it.

validation.error_message: Max message size must be between 1024 (1 KB) and 262144 bytes (256 KB).

inputs.name: max_receive_count
inputs.description: The number of times a message is delivered to the source queue before being moved to the dead-letter queue.

inputs.name: message_retention_seconds
inputs.description: The length of time, in seconds, that Amazon SQS retains a message that does not get deleted.

validation.error_message: Message retention must be between 60 seconds (1 minute) and 1209600 seconds (14 days).

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the SQS queue to be created.

inputs.name: receive_wait_time_seconds
inputs.description: The time for which a ReceiveMessage call will wait for a message to arrive. In other words, the maximum amount of time that polling will wait for messages to become available to receive.

validation.error_message: Receive wait time must be between 0 and 20 seconds.

inputs.name: sqs_managed_sse_enabled
inputs.description: Boolean to enable SQS managed server-side encryption (SSE).

inputs.name: tags
inputs.description: Tags to apply to the SQS queue(s).

inputs.name: visibility_timeout_seconds
inputs.description: The visibility timeout for the queue (in seconds). Visibility timeout sets the length of time that a message received from a queue (by one consumer) will not be visible to the other message consumers.

validation.error_message: Visibility timeout must be between 0 seconds and 43200 seconds (12 hours).

outputs.name: dlq_arn
outputs.description: ARN of the dead-letter queue, if created.

examples.advanced:
module "sqs-queue" {
  source = "github.com/ThiagoPanini/tfbox//aws/sqs-queue"

  name                 = "name-example"
  access_policy        = ""
  access_policy_configuration_type = "file"
  access_policy_file_path = ""
  content_based_deduplication = false
  copy_dlq_config_from_source_queue = false
  create_access_policy = false
  create_dead_letter_queue = true
  create_fifo_queue    = false
}

documents.text: dynamodb-table This Terraform module provisions an AWS DynamoDB table with configurable attributes, billing mode, and key schema. The table's properties such as name, billing mode, hash key, range key, and attribute definitions are parameterized via input database dynamodb

attributes list(map(string)) Information about attributes used in index (hash and range key) of the table in the format [{'name': 'field_name', 'type': 'field_type'}]. All indexes defined for the table (hash key and range key) must mandatorily be contained as elements of this attribute list.
billing_mode string Type of billing associated with consumption of items from the created table
hash_key string Attribute name to be used as primary key (hash key) of the table
name string Name of the table to be created in DynamoDB
range_key string Attribute name to be used as second part of the secondary key (range key) of the table
stream_enabled bool Indicates whether DynamoDB Streams is to be enabled (true) or disabled (false).

stream_view_type string When an item in the table is modified, StreamViewType determines what information is written to the stream for this table. Valid values are: 'NEW_IMAGE', 'OLD_IMAGE', 'NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES', 'KEYS_ONLY'.
tags map(string) Tags to apply to the DynamoDB table.
stream_arn ARN of the DynamoDB table stream.
table_arn ARN of the DynamoDB table.
table_id ID of the DynamoDB table.
table_name Name of the DynamoDB table.

iam-role This Terraform file manages the declaration, rendering, and deployment of IAM policies to be associated with an IAM role. It automates the process of rendering user-defined policy templates, loading the rendered files, and creating AWS IAM  iam dir

policies_arns ARNs of the policies attached to the IAM roles.
roles_arns ARNs of the created IAM roles.

template_dir policies_templates

lambda-function This Terraform module provisions AWS Lambda functions with flexible configuration and best practices. compute cloudwatch lambda resource
architectures list(string) Lambda code architecture (e.g., x86_64, arm64)
create_eventbridge_trigger bool Whether to create an EventBridge rule to trigger the Lambda function based on a cron expression.
cron_expression string Cron expression to schedule the Lambda function using EventBridge (e.g., cron(0 12 * * ? *)).
description string Description of the Lambda function
environment_variables map(string) Map of environment variables to set for the Lambda function.
exclude_patterns list(string) List of glob patterns for files/directories to exclude from source code hash calculation. These files won't trigger Lambda redeployment when changed.
function_name string Name of the Lambda function
lambda_handler string Lambda function handler (e.g., lambda_function.lambda_handler).
layers_arns list(string) List of ARNs for AWS managed Lambda Layers to attach to the function (e.g., AWS provided layers or layers from other accounts).
memory_size number Function memory size in MB from 128 MB to 10240 MB (10 GB)
role_arn string IAM role ARN for Lambda execution
runtime string Lambda runtime (e.g., python3.13, nodejs22.x)
source_code_hash_enabled bool Whether to enable source code hash calculation for change detection. If false, will use timestamp-based triggers (always redeploy).
source_code_path string The local path where the Lambda source code is located (e.g 'app', 'app/src' or 'app/src/my_app_slice').
tags map(string) Tags to apply to the Lambda function.
timeout number Function timeout in seconds
function_arn ARN of the Lambda function
function_name Name of the Lambda function
function_role_arn IAM role ARN used by the Lambda function
aws_cloudwatch_event_rule lambda_event_rule
aws_cloudwatch_event_target lambda_event_target
aws_lambda_permission eventbridge_invoke
aws_lambda_function this
null_resource cleanup_lambda_package
null_resource zip_lambda_package

lambda-layer This Terraform module creates AWS Lambda layers based on the provided configuration. It builds the layers from specified Python requirements, packages them into zip files, and deploys them as Lambda layer versions. The module supports clean compute lambda resource
layers_programming_language string The programming language for the Lambda layers. Currently, only 'python' is supported.
layers_arns A map of Lambda layer ARNs created by this module, with layer names as keys.
aws_lambda_layer_version this
null_resource build_layer
null_resource cleanup_layer_build

sns-topic This Terraform module provisions AWS SNS topics with flexible configuration options. It supports standard and FIFO topics, encryption, topic policies, subscriptions, tags, and data protection policies. messaging sns
content_based_deduplication bool Enable content-based deduplication for FIFO topics.
create_data_protection_policy bool Whether to create a data protection policy.
create_topic_policy bool Whether to create a topic policy.
data_protection_policy string Inline data protection policy JSON.
data_protection_policy_file_path string Path to data protection policy JSON file.
display_name string Display name for the SNS topic.
fifo_topic bool Whether this is a FIFO topic.
kms_master_key_id string KMS key ID for encryption.
name string Name of the SNS topic.
tags map(string) Tags to apply to the SNS topic.

topic_policy_file_path string Path to topic policy JSON file.
topic_arn ARN of the SNS topic.

sqs-queue This Terraform module provisions AWS SQS queues with flexible configuration options. It supports both standard and FIFO queues, optional dead-letter queues (DLQ), encryption (SSE-SQS or KMS), configurable access policies (inline or file-bas messaging sqs
access_policy string The access policy for the SQS queue. This is required if access_policy_configuration_type is 'inline'.
access_policy_configuration_type string Type of access policy configuration. Can be 'inline' or 'file'.
access_policy_file_path string The file path for the access policy if access_policy_configuration_type is 'file'.
content_based_deduplication bool Enables content-based deduplication for FIFO queues.

copy_dlq_config_from_source_queue bool Whether to copy the source queue's configuration to the dead-letter queue. If true, the DLQ will inherit the source queue's settings such as visibility timeout, message retention, etc. If false, users need to configure DLQ settings separately through variables provided.
create_access_policy bool Boolean to enable creation of access policy for the SQS queue.
create_dead_letter_queue bool Whether to create and attach a dead letter queue.
create_fifo_queue bool Boolean to create FIFO queue.
deduplication_scope string The deduplication scope for FIFO queues. Can be 'messageGroup' or 'queue'.

delay_seconds number The time, in seconds, that the delivery of all messages in the queue will be delayed. Any messages sent to the queue remain invisible to consumers for the duration of the delay period
dlq_content_based_deduplication bool Enables content-based deduplication for DLQ FIFO queues.
dlq_deduplication_scope string The deduplication scope for DLQ FIFO queues. Can be 'messageGroup' or 'queue'.

dlq_delay_seconds number The time, in seconds, that the delivery of all messages in the DLQ will be delayed. Any messages sent to the queue remain invisible to consumers for the duration of the delay period
dlq_fifo_throughput_limit string The throughput limit for DLQ FIFO queues. Can be 'perMessageGroupId' or 'perQueue'.
dlq_kms_data_key_reuse_period_seconds number The length of time, in seconds, for which the KMS data key is cached. This is only applicable if KMS master key is used.
dlq_kms_master_key_id string The ID of the KMS master key used for server-side encryption (SSE) if SQS managed SSE is not enabled for DLQ.
dlq_max_message_size number The limit of how many bytes a message can contain before Amazon SQS rejects it.
dlq_message_retention_seconds number The length of time, in seconds, that Amazon SQS retains a message that does not get deleted.

dlq_receive_wait_time_seconds number The time for which a ReceiveMessage call will wait for a message to arrive. In other words, the maximum amount of time that polling will wait for messages to become available to receive.
dlq_sqs_managed_sse_enabled bool Boolean to enable SQS managed server-side encryption (SSE) for DLQ.

dlq_visibility_timeout_seconds number The visibility timeout for the queue (in seconds). Visibility timeout sets the length of time that a message received from a queue (by one consumer) will not be visible to the other message consumers.
fifo_throughput_limit string The throughput limit for FIFO queues. Can be 'perMessageGroupId' or 'perQueue'.
kms_data_key_reuse_period_seconds number The length of time, in seconds, for which the KMS data key is cached. This is only applicable if KMS master key is used.
kms_master_key_id string The ID of the KMS master key used for server-side encryption (SSE) if SQS managed SSE is not enabled.
max_message_size number The limit of how many bytes a message can contain before Amazon SQS rejects it.
max_receive_count number The number of times a message is delivered to the source queue before being moved to the dead-letter queue.
message_retention_seconds number The length of time, in seconds, that Amazon SQS retains a message that does not get deleted.
name string Name of the SQS queue to be created.

receive_wait_time_seconds number The time for which a ReceiveMessage call will wait for a message to arrive. In other words, the maximum amount of time that polling will wait for messages to become available to receive.
sqs_managed_sse_enabled bool Boolean to enable SQS managed server-side encryption (SSE).
tags map(string) Tags to apply to the SQS queue(s).

visibility_timeout_seconds number The visibility timeout for the queue (in seconds). Visibility timeout sets the length of time that a message received from a queue (by one consumer) will not be visible to the other message consumers.
dlq_arn ARN of the dead-letter queue, if created.
queue_arn ARN of the SQS queue.
queue_name Name of the SQS queue.
queue_url URL of the SQS queue.
aws_sqs_queue dlq
aws_sqs_queue source
aws_sqs_queue_redrive_allow_policy dlq_redrive_allow_policy
